purpose: Calculate storage usage against quota policy and determine whether additional writes, uploads, or artifact bundles should be allowed for a given subject, user, tenant, or workspace.

endpoints.description: Evaluate whether a requested write fits within a configured storage quota.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Evaluate storage quota usage before accepting new writes.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before uploads, artifact generation, or bundle assembly that changes persistent storage usage.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as the byte-scanning ingestion layer or as a garbage collection scheduler.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Gather used and requested byte counts. 2) Call /storage/quota/evaluate. 3) Proceed only when allowed is true.

common_calls.description: Check whether a new upload fits quota

failure_modes.condition: Requested write would exceed the quota ceiling
failure_modes.symptom: allowed false with remaining_bytes at zero or below request need
failure_modes.recovery: Reduce stored data, clean up artifacts, or increase the quota before retrying

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Treat quota evaluation as a pure arithmetic policy over current usage and requested bytes, and return projected usage alongside the allow or deny decision.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Quota drift happens when callers pass mixed units or stale usage snapshots, so keep the interface explicitly byte-based and document staleness assumptions.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Return both remaining and projected usage to help UIs and callers explain denials clearly.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not scan the file system or mutate storage state from the quota accountant.
